@@ -2,6 +2,7 @@
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-15l","title":"Add ancestry CLI flag and SQLite storage","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI flag: --show-ancestry (default: false)
- Config key: show_ancestry=true

- Event output with ancestry:
  Pretty: '2026-01-20T10:00:00Z | + connect | anthropic | zsh → tmux → claude (pid=1234) | ...'
  JSON: add 'ancestry': ['zsh(100)', 'tmux(200)', 'claude(1234)']

- SQLite schema update:
  - Add 'ancestry_path' TEXT column to events table
  - Store as comma-separated: 'zsh:100,tmux:200,claude:1234'
  - Index on ancestry_path for queries

- Report enhancement:
  - Group by top-level parent (first in chain)
  - Show: 'Spawned from zsh: 45 connections, tmux: 23 connections'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:20.237572582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:53.478986533Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:53.478919266Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-15l","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:20.308039948Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-15l","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-vb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:17.270912144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-15l","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented --show-ancestry flag + config key (show_ancestry). Event output now includes ancestry chain (pretty) and JSON ancestry array. Added ancestry_path column/index to events and write path as comm:pid chain. Report now includes Spawned From section (top-level parent counts) and JSON ancestry_roots. Note: cargo test -q attempted but was blocked by concurrent cargo lock in shared env.","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1gl","title":"Implement export subcommand","description":"Deliverables:
- Add 'export' to Command enum
- ExportArgs struct with all filter fields
- parse_export_args() function

- run_export() function:
  1. Open SQLite (error if missing)
  2. Build query with WHERE clause from filters
  3. Stream rows to stdout in requested format
  4. Handle Ctrl+C gracefully (don't corrupt output)

- format_csv_row(row) function:
  - Escape quotes by doubling
  - Wrap fields with commas/quotes/newlines in quotes
  - Join with comma, output CRLF

- format_jsonl_row(row) function:
  - Serialize row to JSON object
  - Output with LF

- Memory efficiency:
  - Use SQLite row iteration, not collect()
  - Flush stdout periodically

- Error handling:
  - Missing SQLite file: clear error message
  - Empty result set: output header only (CSV) or nothing (JSONL)
  - Invalid filter: exit with error before query","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:42.648931351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:59:34.850375758Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:59:34.850143029Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1gl","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:42.684768291Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1gl","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-2a6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:57.715021911Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1gl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented export subcommand in src/main.rs (CLI parsing, query builder, CSV/JSONL formatting, streaming + flush, Ctrl+C handling). Added unit tests for csv_escape/glob_to_sql_like/validate_fields. cargo test -q passed.","created_at":"2026-01-21T08:59:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1i6","title":"Connection retry detection","description":"Purpose:
- Detect and highlight reconnection patterns (same remote, repeated connect/close cycles)
- Surface rate limiting, network issues, or bugs in AI clients

User impact:
- See warning: '⚠ Retry pattern: 5 connections to api.anthropic.com:443 in 30s'
- Identify problematic connection behavior without manual analysis
- Correlate retries with provider responses

Features:
- Track connection events per (remote_ip, remote_port, pid)
- Configurable threshold: --retry-threshold <N> (default 3)
- Configurable window: --retry-window-ms <N> (default 60000)
- Output retry warnings inline with events
- Store retry_count in SQLite for analysis

Implementation:
- HashMap<(IpAddr, u16, u32), Vec<Instant>> for recent connections
- On close event: check if count in window exceeds threshold
- Prune old entries on check (sliding window)

Testing:
- Unit tests for retry detection logic
- E2E script that simulates rapid reconnects
- Verify warnings appear and SQLite has retry counts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:56.071844384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:30:25.978010206Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:30:25.977937749Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1i6","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:56.101171213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1jf","title":"Add regression test for top-level help subcommand list","description":"Prevent command discoverability regressions by testing that top-level help output includes implemented subcommands (report/export/diff/status/config/update).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:31.134037985-06:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:20:23.480953972-06:00","closed_at":"2026-02-13T03:20:23.480953972-06:00","close_reason":"Added help-text regression test for subcommand discoverability"}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1jj","title":"Design alert system architecture","description":"Deliverables:
- AlertConfig struct with all threshold fields
- Decision on pattern matching library (regex vs glob vs both)
- Define alert event structure for SQLite storage
- Define alert output format (stderr, prefix, colors)
- Decide on alert state tracking (cooldown to avoid spam)

Design questions to answer:
1. Should alerts be per-connection or per-threshold-breach?
2. How to handle alert cooldown (same alert within N seconds)?
3. Should we track 'alert acknowledged' state?
4. How do alerts interact with --json output mode?

Output:
- Design doc in docs/alert-design.md
- Updated schema proposal for events table","design":"Design decisions (pre-answered to speed implementation):
1. Alerts should be per-connection (each matching event triggers alert, subject to cooldown)
2. Cooldown: HashMap<AlertType, Instant> - skip alert if same type within cooldown_ms
3. No 'acknowledged' state for v1 - keep it simple, user handles alerts
4. JSON mode: alerts as JSON objects on stderr with alert:true field, same structure as events

Pattern matching approach:
- Use glob for simple wildcards (most common case)
- Allow regex with r: prefix for power users (e.g., 'r:.*evil.*')
- Default to glob if no prefix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:28.505557159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:38:54.195422658Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:38:54.195219435Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1jj","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:28.527150866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1ju","title":"Process ancestry chain display","description":"Purpose:
- Show full process tree path for spawned AI processes
- Enable understanding of multi-layer orchestration (shell → tmux → agent → claude)
- Critical for debugging and auditing agent swarms

User impact:
- 'rano --show-ancestry' displays: 'zsh(123) → tmux(456) → bash(789) → claude(1234)'
- Users understand exactly how AI processes were spawned
- Helps identify rogue or unexpected process spawning

Features:
- --show-ancestry flag: include ancestry in event output
- Ancestry format: 'parent_comm(pid) → ... → current_comm(pid)'
- Cache ancestry to avoid repeated /proc walks
- Store ancestry_path in SQLite for historical queries
- Report includes ancestry breakdown

Implementation approach:
- read_ancestry(pid) walks /proc/{pid}/stat PPID chain to init
- Cache: HashMap<pid, Vec<(pid, String)>> with TTL
- Ancestry invalidated if PID recycled (check comm matches)

Testing:
- Unit tests for ancestry chain parsing
- E2E script with nested process tree (shell → script → rano)
- Verify ancestry displayed correctly in output and stored in SQLite","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:18.765913116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:31.933812417Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:31.933767403Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete: ancestry chain reading (bd-vb7), CLI flag + SQLite storage (bd-15l), and tests + docs (bd-94z). Feature fully implemented with 21 unit tests and E2E coverage.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1ju","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:18.871921353Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1lq","title":"Colorblind-friendly themes","description":"Purpose:
- Make rano accessible to users with color vision deficiency
- ~8% of males have some form of color blindness

User impact:
- '--theme colorblind' uses patterns and symbols in addition to colors
- High-contrast palette visible to all users
- Equal usability regardless of color perception

Features:
- New theme: colorblind
- Use unicode markers: ● anthropic, ◆ openai, ▲ google, ○ unknown
- High-contrast palette: blue/orange/black (deuteranopia-friendly)
- Patterns in bar charts: █▓▒░ for differentiation
- Shapes before provider names in stats

Implementation:
- Add theme variants to existing Theme enum
- Map provider to (color, symbol) tuple
- Update format_* functions to include symbol when theme=colorblind

Testing:
- Unit tests for symbol mapping
- E2E script verifying symbols appear in output
- Visual comparison screenshots (docs)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:15.507701965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:05:59.809496537Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T03:05:59.809454899Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1lq","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:15.536896755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
